DOWN doesn't work from the orange's current position. The orange is stuck at rows 33-36, cols 49-52. I've been wasting actions trying to move the orange around the path. Let me reconsider the entire level.
Looking at the image more carefully:
- Top grid: has gray cells (merged greens) with one gray cell in the middle
- Bottom grid: has 3 gray cells and 1 light gray cell
- Orange object on the path
- Purple character at bottom left
Maybe the goal is NOT to move the orange. Maybe I need to click on something in the bottom grid. Or maybe I need to click on the gray cell in the top grid.
Wait, let me look at the bottom grid more carefully. It has 4 cells: 3 gray and 1 light gray (or maybe different shades). Maybe I need to merge the gray cells in the bottom grid like I did with the greens in the top grid.
Let me try clicking on the gray cell in the bottom grid.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Let me look at the bottom grid more carefully
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'g':
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"  Gray: rows {min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, cols {min(cols)}-{max(cols)}, pixels={n['pixels']}, hash={n['hash']}")

# Also check for other colors in the bottom grid area
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if 45 <= n['boundary'][0][0] <= 60:
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"  {n['color']}: rows {min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, cols {min(cols)}-{max(cols)}, pixels={n['pixels']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
